name: Chicago River (Main Stem)
note: Course from the lake at Chicago Harbor Lock west to the Wolf Point confluence; hydraulic regime permanently reversed Jan. 2, 1900 when the Chicago Sanitary & Ship Canal opened. The controlling works/Chicago Harbor Lock at the mouth (which fixes today's lake-end geometry) was built 1936-1938 (docs/02).

name: North Branch Chicago River
note: Sanitary District straightened/relocated this reach (documented for Belmont-Lawrence, 2.2 mi, May 1904-Dec 1907) as part of turn-of-the-century North Side sewer/pumping-station work; other reaches were channelized piecemeal into the early 20th century (docs/08).

name: South Branch Chicago River
note: Modern alignment reflects the Sanitary District's late-1920s South Branch straightening project, which relocated the channel east and eliminated the historic horseshoe bend near Loomis/Damen, completed circa 1930 (task brief; not independently detailed in archive docs beyond the 1900 permanent reversal — see docs/01, docs/02).

name: North Branch Canal (Goose Island)
note: Artificial channel cut on the North Branch's east bank in the 1850s (developer William B. Ogden) to create Goose Island for industrial/dock frontage; exact cutting date not detailed in this archive's docs, so treated as approximate.

name: North Shore Channel
note: Dug 1907-1910 from Lake Michigan at Wilmette south to the North Branch at River Park; completed and put into service November 1910 (docs/02).

name: Chicago Sanitary and Ship Canal
note: Main Channel (Damen Ave to Lockport) built 1892-1900; opened permanently Jan. 2, 1900. Geometry shown is the post-1900 alignment; the parallel Main Channel Extension to Joliet (1903-1907) is included where OSM tags it under the same name (docs/02).

name: Calumet-Saganashkee (Cal-Sag) Channel
note: Original channel dug 1911-1922 (opened Aug 1922) at 60-80 ft width; widened to its modern 225-ft-wide, 9-ft-draft cross-section in a program secondary sources date to 1955-1965/1971, but the 2024 USACE Lockport Water Control Manual instead states the 225-ft widening was completed 'by 1974' -- used here per task instruction; docs/02 flags this as unreconciled.

name: Calumet River
note: Federal channel reached Lake Calumet by 1912 and was deepened/straightened in stages through the 1960s; 1922 is used here as the Calumet Harbor federal project's 'complete with modifications' milestone, the same year the Cal-Sag Channel and Calumet sewage works opened (docs/15).

name: Little Calumet River (Illinois reach)
note: Reach from the Indiana state line west through Blue Island to the Cal-Sag Channel junction; reversed/put under engineered flow control when the Cal-Sag Channel opened in 1922 (docs/02, docs/15).

name: Grand Calumet River (Illinois reach)
note: Small Illinois-side reach near the Indiana state line/Hammond; OSM's 'Grand Calumet River' way is mostly an Indiana feature, so this geometry is clipped at the IL/IN state line (~87.524W) to show only the Illinois portion. This archive's docs do not give a specific channelization date for this segment, so 1922 (the year the wider Calumet system's reversal and harbor works were completed) is used as an approximate marker.

name: Des Plaines River (Lyons-Lockport reach)
note: Reach paralleling the Chicago Sanitary & Ship Canal from Lyons to Lockport; relocated/leveed as part of the Main Channel excavation (1892-1900), whose recorded excavation totals explicitly include 'river-diversion...work' in this corridor (docs/02).

name: Bubbly Creek (South Fork, South Branch)
note: Historic slip serving the Union Stock Yards; OSM tags this waterway 'South Fork South Branch Chicago River' rather than 'Bubbly Creek' (its common name), and its mapped extent is short (~1.5 km) -- much of the historic creek was filled/culverted over the 20th century. Its present truncated extent dates broadly to that industrial-era filling, but this archive's docs (see docs/14) do not give a precise straightening/truncation date, so 1900 is an approximate placeholder for 'from'.

name: Lake Calumet
note: Present ~442-acre outline (2022 CMAP-sourced figure) is the product of a 60-year dredge-and-fill campaign; 1958 marks the last major reshaping documented in the archive (deepening and new slips built for anticipated St. Lawrence Seaway freighter traffic). Sources do not agree on the lake's original or exact current acreage (docs/15).

name: Wolf Lake
note: Present diked, multi-pool outline (5 Illinois impoundments, 3 Indiana) dates to the late 1950s, when the lake was diked so sections could be drained and dredged for Chicago Skyway fill (Skyway opened 1958); a 2007 restoration reset the water-level regime (docs/15).

name: McCook Reservoir / Quarry
note: TARP McCook Reservoir Stage 1 (3.5 BG) finished mining 2016, operational end of 2017; Stage 2 excavation is ongoing (~72% complete as of June 2025, target 2032), so the pit's true final footprint is larger than shown if OSM only reflects an earlier survey (docs/10).

name: Thornton Quarry (Composite Reservoir, north lobe)
note: Quarrying itself dates to the 1830s-1860s (sources disagree, docs/10); the north lobe was converted into the 7.9-BG TARP Thornton Composite Reservoir, excavation complete 2013, reservoir dedicated Sept 1 2015.

name: Salt Creek
note: Receives Egan WRP effluent (upper reach); joins the Des Plaines at Riverside; Westchester PS area drains its lower basin

name: Addison Creek
note: Westchester-area receiving stream (Salt Creek tributary)

name: Weller Creek
note: Kirie CSO outfall 111 receiving stream (via Upper Des Plaines TARP)

name: Higgins Creek
note: Receives Kirie WRP effluent (NPDES Outfall 001 at 42.0194,-87.9367); flows east through the O'Hare area and joins Willow Creek at 42.00665,-87.92357 — the combined flow continues under the name Willow Creek to the Des Plaines River at Rosemont. Downstream reach traced from OSM, cross-validated against MWRD Waterways GIS (0-21 m).

name: West Branch DuPage River
note: Receives Hanover Park WRP effluent; reach southward through DuPage County

name: Skokie River (East Fork, North Branch Chicago River)
note: Originally the Skokie marsh; channelized early 20th c.; the CCC excavated the 7-pool Skokie Lagoons (1933-42) mid-course (lagoon reach ~42.134 to ~42.102 N). Carries NSWRD Clavey Road WRF effluent (piped in below Dundee Rd since the post-1979 lagoons diversion) south past the Chicago Botanic Garden reach and lagoons to the head of our mapped North Branch at the Skokie/North Branch naming boundary; the West Fork joins farther south at 42.0523,-87.7805. Centerline: OSM 17-way chain cross-validated against MWRD Waterways GIS (0-5.5 m agreement).

name: Willow Creek (carries Higgins Creek flow to the Des Plaines)
note: Willow Creek from the Higgins Creek junction (42.00665,-87.92357) to its Des Plaines River confluence at Rosemont (41.98791,-87.85550). OSM naming: 'Higgins Creek' ends at the junction; MWRD GIS shows identical topology. Also receives pumped stormwater from O'Hare's north-airfield CDA detention basin (a separate, non-TARP city system).
